Dementia Status Prediction

Jessica Ho

2025-03-20

library("epiR")

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tibble    3.3.1

## ✖ ggplot2::%+%()     masks psych::%+%()

## ✖ dplyr::combine()   masks gridExtra::combine()

library(caret)         
## Loading required package: lattice
## 
## Attaching package: 'lattice'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:boot':
## 
##     melanoma

## The following object is masked from 'package:epiDisplay':

## 
##

##     lift

## The following object is masked from 'package:survival':
## 
##     cluster
library(rpart)         
## 
## Attaching package: 'rpart'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:marginaleffects':
## 
##     prune
library(rpart.plot)    
library(cluster)      
library(factoextra) 
## Welcome to factoextra!
## Want to learn more? See two factoextra-related books at https://www.datanovia.com/en/product/practical-guide-to-principal-component-methods-in-r/
df <- read.csv("DonorInformation.csv")
str(df)
## 'data.frame':    107 obs. of  19 variables:
##  $ donor_id                 : int  326765665 326765656 326765654 467056391 309335447 309335457 326765676 326765681 326765648 326765657 ...
##  $ name                     : chr  "H14.09.078" "H14.09.069" "H14.09.067" "H15.09.103" ...
##  $ age                      : chr  "87" "95-99" "85" "90-94" ...
##  $ sex                      : chr  "M" "M" "M" "F" ...
##  $ apo_e4_allele            : chr  "N" "N" "Y" "N" ...
##  $ education_years          : int  16 17 10 11 16 18 12 18 16 16 ...
##  $ age_at_first_tbi         : int  0 12 72 87 0 0 13 0 6 0 ...
##  $ longest_loc_duration     : chr  "Unknown or N/A" "1-2 min" "< 10 sec" "< 10 sec" ...
##  $ cerad                    : int  0 2 3 0 3 2 3 1 1 0 ...
##  $ num_tbi_w_loc            : int  0 1 1 1 0 0 1 0 1 0 ...
##  $ dsm_iv_clinical_diagnosis: chr  "No Dementia" "No Dementia" "Vascular" "No Dementia" ...
##  $ control_set              : int  31 26 25 52 28 1 43 46 22 26 ...
##  $ nincds_arda_diagnosis    : chr  "No Dementia" "No Dementia" "Dementia, Type Unknown" "No Dementia" ...
##  $ ever_tbi_w_loc           : chr  "N" "Y" "Y" "Y" ...
##  $ race                     : chr  "White" "White" "White" "White" ...
##  $ hispanic                 : chr  "Not Hispanic" "Not Hispanic" "Not Hispanic" "Not Hispanic" ...
##  $ act_demented             : chr  "No Dementia" "No Dementia" "Dementia" "No Dementia" ...
##  $ braak                    : int  1 5 4 4 4 3 6 2 2 2 ...
##  $ nia_reagan               : int  1 2 2 0 2 2 3 1 1 1 ...
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    apo_e4_allele = as.factor(apo_e4_allele),
    act_demented = as.factor(act_demented),  # Target variable
    ever_tbi_w_loc = as.factor(ever_tbi_w_loc),
    control_set = as.factor(control_set)
  )

df$act_demented <- factor(df$act_demented, levels = c("No Dementia", "Dementia"))


# Remove rows where apo_e4_allele is "N/A"
df <- df[df$apo_e4_allele != "N/A", ]


# converting age variable into numeric (taking the midpoint of range levels)
df <- df %>%
  mutate(age_numeric = case_when(
    age == "100+"  ~ 100,   
    age == "90-94" ~ 92,   
    age == "95-99" ~ 97, 
    TRUE ~ as.numeric(as.character(age))  # convert numbers directly
  ))
## Warning: There was 1 warning in `mutate()`.
## ℹ In argument: `age_numeric = case_when(...)`.
## Caused by warning:
## ! NAs introduced by coercion
set.seed(123)  
train_ind <- sample(nrow(df), size = 0.7 * nrow(df), replace = FALSE)
train_data <- df[train_ind, ]
test_data <- df[-train_ind, ]


logit_model <- glm(act_demented ~ age_numeric + education_years + apo_e4_allele + num_tbi_w_loc + braak + cerad, data = train_data, family = "binomial")

summary(logit_model)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = act_demented ~ age_numeric + education_years + 
##     apo_e4_allele + num_tbi_w_loc + braak + cerad, family = "binomial", 
##     data = train_data)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                 Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)  
## (Intercept)      1.28771    4.59977   0.280   0.7795  
## age_numeric     -0.01907    0.04689  -0.407   0.6842  
## education_years -0.09528    0.08955  -1.064   0.2873  
## apo_e4_alleleY   0.98720    0.66402   1.487   0.1371  
## num_tbi_w_loc    0.01491    0.43756   0.034   0.9728  
## braak            0.37719    0.20310   1.857   0.0633 .
## cerad            0.02326    0.29717   0.078   0.9376  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 95.607  on 69  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 84.963  on 63  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 98.963
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4
# odds ratios
or <- exp(coef(logit_model))
or
##     (Intercept)     age_numeric education_years  apo_e4_alleleY   num_tbi_w_loc 
##       3.6244812       0.9811091       0.9091198       2.6837189       1.0150247 
##           braak           cerad 
##       1.4581865       1.0235296
or_ci <- exp(confint(logit_model))
## Waiting for profiling to be done...
or_ci
##                        2.5 %       97.5 %
## (Intercept)     0.0004707891 41494.213474
## age_numeric     0.8916436739     1.074590
## education_years 0.7564328573     1.079748
## apo_e4_alleleY  0.7462546598    10.538814
## num_tbi_w_loc   0.4176990825     2.375091
## braak           0.9925738710     2.225666
## cerad           0.5663123610     1.840795
library(sjPlot)
## 
## Attaching package: 'sjPlot'
## The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     set_theme
t2 <- tab_model(logit_model, 
                title = "Table 1 - Odds Ratios of Predictors of Dementia Status",
                show.p = TRUE,
                show.obs = TRUE,
                show.reflvl = FALSE,
                show.re.var = TRUE)
# TRAINING DATA

# predict probabilities
logit_probs_train <- predict(logit_model, train_data, type = "response")

# binary predictions
logit_preds_train <- ifelse(logit_probs_train > 0.5, "Dementia", "No Dementia")

# factors for confusion matrix
logit_preds_train <- factor(logit_preds_train, levels = levels(train_data$act_demented))

confusionMatrix(logit_preds_train, train_data$act_demented)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##              Reference
## Prediction    No Dementia Dementia
##   No Dementia          29       15
##   Dementia             11       15
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.6286          
##                  95% CI : (0.5048, 0.7411)
##     No Information Rate : 0.5714          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.1995          
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.2288          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.5563          
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.7250          
##             Specificity : 0.5000          
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.6591          
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.5769          
##              Prevalence : 0.5714          
##          Detection Rate : 0.4143          
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.6286          
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.6125          
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : No Dementia     
## 
# con_mat <- table(True = train_data$act_demented, Predicted = logit_preds_train)
# con_mat
#
# # Total correctly classified (TP + TN)
# correctly_classified_train <- sum(diag(con_mat))
# 
# # Total number of observations
# total_observation_train <- sum(con_mat)
# 
# # Proportion of correctly classified observations
# accuracy_train <- correctly_classified_train / total_observation_train
# cat("Classification accuracy on training data:", accuracy_train)
# TEST DATA

logit_probs_test <- predict(logit_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response")

logit_preds_test <- ifelse(logit_probs_test > 0.5, "Dementia", "No Dementia")

logit_preds_test <- factor(logit_preds_test, levels = levels(test_data$act_demented))

confusionMatrix(logit_preds_test, test_data$act_demented)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##              Reference
## Prediction    No Dementia Dementia
##   No Dementia          11        6
##   Dementia              4        9
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.6667          
##                  95% CI : (0.4719, 0.8271)
##     No Information Rate : 0.5             
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.04937         
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.3333          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.75183         
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.7333          
##             Specificity : 0.6000          
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.6471          
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.6923          
##              Prevalence : 0.5000          
##          Detection Rate : 0.3667          
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.5667          
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.6667          
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : No Dementia     
## 
# conf_matrix <- table(True = test_data$act_demented, Predicted = logit_preds_test)
# conf_matrix
# # Total correctly classified (TP + TN)
# correctly_classified <- sum(diag(conf_matrix))
# 
# # Total number of observations
# total_observations <- sum(conf_matrix)
# 
# # Proportion of correctly classified observations
# accuracy <- correctly_classified / total_observations
# cat("Classification accuracy on test data:", accuracy)
library(pROC)
## Type 'citation("pROC")' for a citation.
## 
## Attaching package: 'pROC'
## The following object is masked from 'package:epiDisplay':
## 
##     ci
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     cov, smooth, var
# act_demented is binary (0 and 1) for ROC analysis
test_data$act_demented_binary <- ifelse(test_data$act_demented == "Dementia", 1, 0)

roc_curve <- roc(test_data$act_demented_binary, logit_probs_test)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
# Plot ROC Curve
plot(roc_curve, col = "blue", lwd = 2, main = "ROC Curve for Logistic Regression Model")

# AUC Value
auc_value <- auc(roc_curve)
print(paste("AUC:", auc_value))
## [1] "AUC: 0.724444444444444"
library(randomForest)
## randomForest 4.7-1.2
## Type rfNews() to see new features/changes/bug fixes.
## 
## Attaching package: 'randomForest'
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     combine
## The following object is masked from 'package:gridExtra':
## 
##     combine
## The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     margin
## The following object is masked from 'package:psych':
## 
##     outlier
set.seed(1)
best_mtry <- tuneRF(train_data[, c("age_numeric", "education_years", "apo_e4_allele", "num_tbi_w_loc", "braak", "cerad")],
                    train_data$act_demented,
                    stepFactor = 1.5,  # Increases `mtry` in steps
                    improve = 0.01,   # Stop if improvement < 1%
                    trace = TRUE)
## mtry = 2  OOB error = 41.43% 
## Searching left ...
## Searching right ...
## mtry = 3     OOB error = 38.57% 
## 0.06896552 0.01 
## mtry = 4     OOB error = 41.43% 
## -0.07407407 0.01

ntree_values <- c(100, 300, 500, 700, 1000)
results <- data.frame(ntree = integer(), OOBError = numeric())

for (n in ntree_values) {
  rf_test <- randomForest(
    act_demented ~ age_numeric + education_years + apo_e4_allele + num_tbi_w_loc + braak + cerad,
    data = train_data,
    ntree = n,
    mtry = 3
  )
  
  # Store OOB error
  results <- rbind(results, data.frame(ntree = n, OOBError = rf_test$err.rate[n, 1]))
}

results
##      ntree  OOBError
## OOB    100 0.4714286
## OOB1   300 0.4285714
## OOB2   500 0.4285714
## OOB3   700 0.4428571
## OOB4  1000 0.4428571
# Train a Random Forest model
set.seed(1)
rf_model <- randomForest(act_demented ~ age_numeric + education_years + apo_e4_allele + num_tbi_w_loc + braak + cerad, data = train_data, ntree = 500, mtry = 3, importance = TRUE)

rf_model
## 
## Call:
##  randomForest(formula = act_demented ~ age_numeric + education_years +      apo_e4_allele + num_tbi_w_loc + braak + cerad, data = train_data,      ntree = 500, mtry = 3, importance = TRUE) 
##                Type of random forest: classification
##                      Number of trees: 500
## No. of variables tried at each split: 3
## 
##         OOB estimate of  error rate: 44.29%
## Confusion matrix:
##             No Dementia Dementia class.error
## No Dementia          27       13       0.325
## Dementia             18       12       0.600
importance <- importance(rf_model)

# sort the importance values
importance_sorted <- importance[order(importance[,1], decreasing = TRUE),]
importance_sorted
##                 No Dementia    Dementia MeanDecreaseAccuracy MeanDecreaseGini
## braak            7.28302990  8.64352121           11.2492511         8.041049
## education_years  2.88745142 -3.19246262            0.3472801         7.467544
## age_numeric      2.29291971 -1.19535540            1.1461014         8.350365
## apo_e4_allele    1.70537199 -4.32428138           -1.5580193         1.590221
## cerad            0.01707915  4.82580733            3.2394571         5.106331
## num_tbi_w_loc   -3.67329925 -0.09750411           -2.9892803         2.375186
# feature importance plot
varImpPlot(rf_model)

rf_preds <- predict(rf_model, test_data)

conf_matrix_rf <- confusionMatrix(rf_preds, test_data$act_demented)
conf_matrix_rf
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##              Reference
## Prediction    No Dementia Dementia
##   No Dementia          11        3
##   Dementia              4       12
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.7667          
##                  95% CI : (0.5772, 0.9007)
##     No Information Rate : 0.5             
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.002611        
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.5333          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 1.000000        
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.7333          
##             Specificity : 0.8000          
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.7857          
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.7500          
##              Prevalence : 0.5000          
##          Detection Rate : 0.3667          
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.4667          
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.7667          
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : No Dementia     
## 
rf_probs <- predict(rf_model, test_data, type = "prob")[, "Dementia"]

roc_curve <- roc(test_data$act_demented, rf_probs)
## Setting levels: control = No Dementia, case = Dementia
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_curve, col = "blue", main = "ROC Curve for Random Forest")

 auc(roc_curve)
## Area under the curve: 0.8
#KMEANS
cluster_data <- df %>%
  select(age_numeric, education_years, num_tbi_w_loc, braak, cerad) %>%
  na.omit()

# scale for better clustering performance
cluster_scaled <- scale(cluster_data)

fviz_nbclust(cluster_scaled, kmeans, method = "wss") 

fviz_nbclust(cluster_scaled, kmeans, method = "silhouette")

set.seed(123)

km_res <- kmeans(cluster_data, centers = 2, nstart = 15)
fviz_cluster(km_res, 
             stand=FALSE,
             show.clust.cent=TRUE, 
             ellipse.type = "norm",
             data = cluster_data)

df$cluster <- as.factor(km_res$cluster)

table(df$cluster, df$apo_e4_allele)
##    
##      N N/A  Y
##   1 35   0 11
##   2 45   0  9
prop.table(table(df$cluster, df$apo_e4_allele), margin = 1)
##    
##             N       N/A         Y
##   1 0.7608696 0.0000000 0.2391304
##   2 0.8333333 0.0000000 0.1666667
# bar plot of apo_e4_allele distribution across clusters
ggplot(df, aes(x = cluster, fill = apo_e4_allele)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of ApoE4 Allele Across Clusters",
       x = "Cluster", y = "Proportion") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2")
